title: hieracium froelichianum subsp. crepididens
intro: Hieracium froelichianum subsp. crepididens, also known as hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow flowers and jagged leaves. Traditionally, hawkweed has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of hawkweed include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Hawkweed can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used topically to treat skin issues or taken internally to promote overall well-being.
